title: === and !== should be preferred instead of == and !=
description: In JavaScript, there are two types of comparison operators: strict and non-strict. Strict operators: These operators compare both value and type. They are represented as === (strict equality) and !== (strict inequality).
recommendation: Use strict equality operators (=== and !==) instead of loose equality operators (== and !=).
impact: Code quality and potential type coercion bugs

checks.message: Use strict equality (===) instead of loose equality (==)
